overview: Bapatla College of Pharmacy (BCOP), established in 1995, is a premier pharmacy institution in Andhra Pradesh. It is managed by the Bapatla Education Society and focuses on providing quality education in pharmaceutical sciences to rural and urban students alike.
accreditation: Approved by the Pharmacy Council of India (PCI) and the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE). Affiliated with Acharya Nagarjuna University.

coursesAndFees.eligibility: 10+2 with 45% aggregate in PCB/PCM + AP EAPCET

admissions.admissionProcess: Admission is primarily based on state-level entrance exam ranks (AP EAPCET for UG, AP PGECET for PG) followed by web counseling. Management quota seats are filled according to APSCHE guidelines.
admissions.importantDates: AP EAPCET 2026 Pharmacy Exam: May 19 - May 20, 2026; AP PGECET 2026 Exam: Early May 2026.

campus.specialNote: The campus is known for its serene, green environment and high-quality lab equipment. While there is a girls' hostel on campus, boys typically utilize private hostels in Bapatla town.

contact.address: Kothapalem, Bapatla, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh - 522101
